title: Staurogyne polybotrya
taxonomy: Staurogyne polybotrya belongs to the family Acanthaceae, a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as acanthus or acanthus family. This family includes around 2,684 species, spread across 320 genera. Staurogyne polybotrya is further classified as a member of the genus Staurogyne, which comprises about 63 species of flowering plants. The species is also classified as a type of shrub or herb, native to tropical regions in Central and South America. Its classification into the family Acanthaceae is due to its characteristic growth habit, leaf shape, and flower structure.
uses: Staurogyne polybotrya has been traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in the Amazon region, for various purposes. Its medicinal properties include antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities.
